evals.prompt: I want to start a new dbt-confluent project from scratch. I've already got a Confluent Cloud env, a Kafka cluster called 'orders-cluster', a Flink compute pool, and a service-account Flink API key. The source data is an existing Kafka topic 'orders' in the 'orders-cluster'. I want a 5-minute tumbling window of order counts and total revenue per customer.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolded project with dbt_project.yml (streaming_table default), profiles.yml.example, requirements.txt with a verified dbt-confluent pin, .gitignore, models/sources.yml declaring the existing orders topic, models/models.yml with column types and constraints for the new streaming_table, and models/<model>.sql containing the windowed aggregation using the TVF TUMBLE form. The warning-comment block is at the top of the streaming model. The user is told to run dbt commands themselves (not autonomous).
expectations: Confirms the detected workflow (1 — scaffold) via AskUserQuestion before generating files
Asks pre-flight question and acknowledges the user already has the Confluent Cloud prerequisites — skips to scaffolding
Verifies the published dbt-confluent version against PyPI (e.g. via WebFetch on https://pypi.org/pypi/dbt-confluent/json) before pinning in requirements.txt
Generates a models/sources.yml entry for the existing 'orders' topic with database: <env-id>, schema: orders-cluster — NOT a streaming_source
Does NOT generate a streaming_source for the existing 'orders' topic (existing topics use dbt sources)
Generates a models/models.yml entry with column types for the new streaming_table model (required, not optional)
Streaming-table model SQL uses the TVF form: FROM TABLE(TUMBLE(TABLE ..., DESCRIPTOR(`$rowtime`), INTERVAL '5' MINUTES)) — not the legacy GROUP BY tumble(...) form
Uses backticks for identifiers including `$rowtime`, `window_start`, `window_end`
Streaming-table model SQL starts with the verbatim warning comment block about running --full-refresh after editing the query
dbt_project.yml sets +materialized: streaming_table as the project default
.gitignore includes profiles.yml, target/, dbt_packages/, logs/, .env
profiles.yml.example uses environment_id (aliased to database) and dbname (aliased to schema), with cloud_provider+cloud_region or endpoint
Does NOT autonomously run `dbt run` or `dbt run --full-refresh` — presents these as user-runnable steps
Recommends a service-account-bound Flink API key for prod targets

evals.prompt: Add an incremental dbt model to my dbt-confluent project that updates a running per-user order total. I want efficient incremental updates instead of recomputing every run.
evals.expected_output: Explains that `incremental` is not supported by dbt-confluent and would raise a compile error. Routes the user to `streaming_table`, which is continuously updated by Flink — exactly the 'incremental updates without recompute' mental model the user wants. Generates a streaming_table model with the required warning comment block and a matching models.yml entry.
expectations: Does NOT generate an incremental materialization — explicitly explains it raises a compilation error in dbt-confluent
Explains that `streaming_table` is the right answer because Flink continuously updates the underlying table — no recompute needed
Generates a streaming_table model with the verbatim warning comment block at the top
Generates a matching models.yml entry with column types and any not_null/primary_key constraints (constraints in the constraints: list, NOT in data_type)
Uses backticks for identifiers in the model SQL
Does NOT suggest `materialized_view` — explains that in Confluent Flink, `table` (CTAS) is continuously updated by Flink
Does NOT autonomously run `dbt run`

evals.prompt: I edited the WHERE clause on my streaming_table model `orders_per_5m` yesterday to filter out test customers. I ran `dbt run --select orders_per_5m` and it said SKIP. The change isn't showing up in the output topic. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the silent-failure mode in schema-drift detection: changes to query logic (WHERE clause) that don't shift column names/types are not detected, and `dbt run` SKIPs. The fix is `dbt run --full-refresh --select orders_per_5m`. Surfaces this as the universal cause for 'I edited a streaming model and nothing changed.' Does NOT autonomously run --full-refresh — suggests it to the user.
expectations: Identifies that schema-drift detection only checks column names, data types, and WITH options — not query logic
Explains that a WHERE-clause change is exactly the silent-failure case: no drift detected, no error, no redeploy
Suggests `dbt run --full-refresh --select orders_per_5m` as the fix
Does NOT autonomously run `dbt run --full-refresh` — surfaces that this is a state-changing command the user must run
Mentions or implies that --full-refresh will delete the existing Flink statement and recreate the table
Does NOT recommend ALTER TABLE or any other workaround — the only mechanism is --full-refresh

evals.prompt: I'm running `dbt test --select orders_per_5m` on my streaming_table model with a not_null test on `customer_id`. The test passes, but I just spotted a null customer_id in the output topic in the UI. Why is the test missing it?
evals.expected_output: Identifies that data tests on unbounded streaming tables are unreliable: the cursor returns at most a 1000-row partial snapshot, so a not_null violation that hasn't appeared yet passes spuriously. Suggests mitigations in order: bound the source (number-of-rows on streaming_source), use config(limit=N), or prefer unit tests with controlled fixtures. Explains that unit tests need the model to exist first (`dbt run --select my_model && dbt test --select unit_my_model`).
expectations: Identifies the partial-snapshot / 1000-row cursor cap on unbounded streaming statements as the cause
Explains the spurious pass: a violation that hasn't appeared in the snapshot passes — does not mean the data is clean
Suggests bounding the source (e.g. `number-of-rows` on a streaming_source) OR using `config(limit=N)` on the test OR using unit tests
Mentions that unit tests use a custom materialization (CREATE TABLE LIKE ... EXCLUDING OPTIONS + INSERT) — not CTEs — because Flink CTEs can't carry watermarks
Mentions that the tested model must already exist before `dbt test --select unit_<model>` runs
Does NOT suggest standard data tests are reliable here without bounding

evals.prompt: Write me a streaming_source model that pulls from a 'page_views' Kafka topic that already exists in my cluster. I want to read it and aggregate.
evals.expected_output: Pushes back: `streaming_source` is for adapter-CREATED connector-backed tables, not for read-only references to topics produced elsewhere. For an existing topic, use a dbt `source` (the topic auto-appears as a Flink table). Generates a `sources.yml` entry for `page_views` and a separate `streaming_table` (or `table`) model that references it via `{{ source('...', 'page_views') }}`.
expectations: Does NOT generate a streaming_source for the existing 'page_views' topic
Explains the distinction: streaming_source CREATES a connector-backed table; existing topics are accessed via dbt sources (every Kafka topic is automatically a Flink table)
Generates a sources.yml entry with database: <env-id>, schema: <cluster>, tables: [{name: page_views}]
Generates the aggregation model as either `streaming_table` or `table`, referencing the source via {{ source('<source_name>', 'page_views') }}
If the aggregation is a streaming_table, includes the verbatim warning comment block and a matching models.yml entry
Uses backticks for identifiers

evals.prompt: Quick question — what does config(with={'changelog.mode': 'upsert'}) do, and when should I use it?
evals.expected_output: Brief explanation: upsert mode is last-value-per-key (requires a PRIMARY KEY); append is insert-only. Downstream consumers see different streams. Default depends on whether the table has a PK. Picks the right mode deliberately. No scaffolding needed — this is a one-off question per the SKILL.md router.
expectations: Treats this as a one-off question (no workflow scaffolding, no AskUserQuestion confirming a workflow)
Explains append vs upsert succinctly: append = insert-only; upsert = last-value-per-key, requires PRIMARY KEY
Mentions that the choice changes what downstream consumers see — not interchangeable
Notes that PRIMARY KEY in Flink is NOT ENFORCED (metadata for downstream operators, not a unique constraint at write time)
Does NOT scaffold files or run commands
